description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden frame and fabric-covered wings, featuring a dual-wing structure with prominent struts and rigging wires. The wings are straight and parallel, each bearing roundels with red, white, and blue concentric circles, suggesting a military or historical design. The fuselage is slender and open, with an exposed cockpit situated near the front, accommodating two seats in tandem. The engine is mounted at the nose, with a visible propeller and a pair of fixed landing skids and wheels beneath the lower wing. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer, both adorned with red, white, and blue stripes. The overall appearance is reminiscent of early 20th-century reconnaissance or trainer aircraft, with a lightweight and utilitarian construction.
description: A vintage wooden biplane with dual wings, open tandem cockpit, and twin vertical stabilizers, featuring military roundels and a front-mounted propeller engine.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the open cockpit, nose-mounted engine with propeller, and the dual landing skids and wheels.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal tailplane, and the rigging between the wings and tail.
description_view_2: Rear-right view emphasizing the tail assembly, twin stabilizers, and the structure of the biplane's wings and rigging.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the cockpit, engine, and the arrangement of the landing gear and skids.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with two parallel, straight wings connected by struts and rigging; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a front propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear with two main wheels and dual skids; no retractable or tail gear visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open tandem cockpit with two seats, no canopy or enclosed structure.
